title: UK and Florida commit $400,000 to joint space projects
summary: The UK and Florida have each pledged $200,000 to fund joint space research, innovation, and commercialization projects. This collaboration aims to strengthen ties between the two regions in the space industry.

title: German component supplier deltaVision raises 10.2 million euros
summary: German spacecraft component developer deltaVision raised 10.2 million euros to expand production of valves and related equipment, focusing on enabling in-space refueling.

title: Agile Space Industries Appoints new CTO and Board member.
summary: Agile Space Industries has appointed Jake Mills as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO) and Mark Pasquale as an independent board member. The appointments were announced on July 21, 2026.

title: KBR organizes defense technology business to pursue Golden Dome work
summary: KBR has established a dedicated defense technology business unit to focus on the Golden Dome program, leveraging its expertise in digital engineering, space tracking, and missile defense. The move aims to strengthen its position in the defense and space sectors.

title: IHI explores Kuva hyperspectral satellites for Japan’s multi-sensor constellation
summary: IHI is evaluating Kuva Space's next-generation hyperspectral satellites for inclusion in Japan's planned 100-satellite Earth observation constellation. The move highlights Japan's push for a multi-sensor sovereign satellite network.

title: Italian startup ORiS raises funding for laser power-beaming technology
summary: Italian startup ORiS has secured 5 million euros ($5.7 million) in funding to advance its laser-based wireless power-beaming technology for satellites. The innovation aims to enable energy transmission to spacecraft without physical connections.

title: Pipelines to payloads: risk management lessons for the space economy
summary: The article draws parallels between risk management in the energy sector and the emerging space economy, highlighting complex disputes involving sovereign power, geopolitics, regulation, and private capital. It suggests that lessons from the energy industry can inform the space sector's growth and challenges.

title: AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raises $29.5 million angel round
summary: Chinese AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raised $29.5 million in an angel funding round. The six-month-old company aims to use AI to accelerate rocket engine development.

title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary: Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords on July 17, becoming the 70th nation to join the international agreement in under six years.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary: The U.S. Space Force increased the maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract from $5.6 billion to $17 billion to meet rising demand. This move reflects growing needs in national security space missions.

title: SpaceX will launch a satellite repair drone with robot arms to Earth orbit today: How to watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch a satellite repair drone equipped with robotic arms into Earth orbit today. The mission marks a significant advancement in satellite servicing technology.

title: A massive crater was spotted on Google Earth. It could be a scar from an ancient meteorite impact
summary: An amateur astronomer discovered a massive crater on Google Earth that may be the scar of an ancient meteorite impact. Microscopic analysis is pending to confirm the findings.

title: Galaxy cluster's magnetic field reconstructed for 1st time with record-breaking astronomy map
summary: Astronomers have reconstructed the magnetic field of a galaxy cluster for the first time using a record-breaking astronomy map. The mechanism behind the 'turning on' of these gigantic radio emissions is believed to be linked to the formation process of galaxy clusters.

title: The 1st 'Avengers: Doomsday' trailer is here, and it's stuffed to bursting with superheroes
summary: The first trailer for 'Avengers: Doomsday' has been released, featuring Robert Downey Jr.'s Doctor Doom in a highly anticipated debut. The trailer is packed with numerous superheroes ahead of the December blockbuster release.

title: SpaceX targets July 23 for crucial Starship Flight 13 test launch after abort
summary: SpaceX has rescheduled the Flight 13 test launch of its Starship rocket to July 23 after an abort at the last second on July 16. The company aims to proceed with the crucial test following the initial delay.

title: Scientists discover mystery molecule on Pluto and Saturn's moon Titan: 'We cannot say what it is'
summary: Scientists have identified a mysterious molecule on Pluto and Saturn's moon Titan, separated by billions of miles, but its exact nature remains unknown. The unexplained surface signature suggests a shared yet unidentified chemical process.

title: 50 years after Viking 1's historic Mars landing, NASA is looking to the Red Planet's skies
summary: NASA commemorates the 50th anniversary of Viking 1's Mars landing, marking a historic milestone in space exploration. The agency now focuses on future aerial missions to explore the Red Planet's atmosphere.

title: 'Stranger Things' at 10: The Netflix streaming icon that made it cool to like science again
summary: Stranger Things has significantly influenced streaming culture and reignited younger audiences' interest in science over the past decade. The show's blend of science fiction and nostalgia made science more appealing to a new generation.

title: Twice in a row: SpaceX rocket aborts launch at last second again (video)
summary: SpaceX experienced its second consecutive last-second abort during a Falcon 9 launch attempt for Starlink broadband satellites on July 20. The incident occurred just before the scheduled liftoff.

title: Four astronauts, 3 spacecraft, 2 weeks in orbit: Artemis III will be NASA's most complicated mission ever
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission is set to be the most complex in history, involving the coordination of three spacecraft and four astronauts over two weeks in orbit. The mission aims to achieve a historic lunar landing as part of the Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new attempt at launching Starship Flight 13 after a previous aborted launch. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are scheduled for upcoming launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Three companies—Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space—are making progress on their respective launch vehicles: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5. Despite recent attention on Starship’s test program, these rockets represent significant advancements in the space industry.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition when Booster 20 failed to ignite properly. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was called off due to the abort.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission to sustain operations and research aboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step before its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the company's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing rapid development with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks a significant step in the region's ongoing revitalization.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, though specific details are not provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights significant milestones and contributions in space exploration and science.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: ESA's PLATO spacecraft has completed key electromagnetic compatibility tests in the Maxwell Test Chamber, confirming its electronics are space-ready. The mission is now cleared for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in March 2027.

title: The art of hadrons
summary: The news headline 'The art of hadrons' suggests a creative or artistic representation of hadrons, which are subatomic particles. The accompanying image likely visualizes this concept artistically.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, focusing on planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and industry stakeholders.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has supported the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This mission is part of Prodex's efforts to enable European research institutes to participate in space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will be used to expand manufacturing capacity and research new capabilities.

title: UK Commits £62M into Domestic Space Capabilities
summary: The UK government committed £62 million ($83.3 million) to enhance domestic satellite communications and space technologies. This investment aims to bolster the UK's space capabilities.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, becoming the first private Indian company to reach orbit. The launch took place from India’s Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contract ceiling by $11.4 billion, tripling the total pool from $5.6 billion to $17 billion. This move expands the National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program's funding for future missions.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs secured a $10 million seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This partnership aims to enhance communication capabilities for the lunar mission.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the organization's liquidation and future plans. The interview explores the vision for ISU beyond its current challenges.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has secured a $70 million Series C funding round to expand its operations and meet the growing demand for sovereign space hardware. The investment will support the company's scaling efforts in the space industry.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed framework for mission authorization, questioning its financial feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns about the office's ability to manage the new framework.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The news discusses concerns about a potential sales freeze for SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which could disrupt the space industry's reliance on its reliable launch services. This uncertainty raises existential questions for many satellite businesses.

title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
summary: The UK government will invest an additional £62 million ($83.4 million) to strengthen the country's space sector, focusing on new satellite communications systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully executed India's first private orbital launch with its Vikram-1 rocket. This marks a historic milestone for the country's space industry.

title: ThinKom ThinAir GT Antenna Secures Government Certification for O3b mPower
summary: ThinKom's ThinAir GT2517 terminal has become the first airborne antenna to receive SES's Government Technology Certification for use on the O3b mPOWER constellation. This Ka-band terminal enables secure government communications via SES's satellite network.

title: Spire Hires Mantech Exec Eric Mellinger as COO
summary: Spire Global has appointed Eric Mellinger, a former Mantech International executive, as its Chief Operating Officer to drive business development and go-to-market strategy. Mellinger brings nearly eight years of experience from his previous role at Mantech.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on lunar exploration. The partnership involves exchanging technical information and conducting system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Federal LLC secured SpaceWERX funding to advance its quantum-assisted AI platform for improving space domain awareness (SDA), marking the company's first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes developing space-based moving target indication systems and countering Chinese military space systems as key deterrence goals. He emphasizes these capabilities as critical for the Space Force's future.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the emerging industry of space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit. Industry players debated the feasibility and potential of orbital compute stacks.
